title: paraprasophyllum robustum
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum robustum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to its volatile oils. Ingestion of the plant's parts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while long-term exposure may result in liver and kidney damage. Some individuals may experience headaches, dizziness, and fatigue after coming into contact with the plant, while others may suffer from increased heart rate and blood pressure. Improper use of Paraprasophyllum robustum can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
